tree.setup: You're at a street market looking at a scarf.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这个围巾很好看！五十块。
turns.text_pinyin: zhè ge wéijīn hěn hǎokàn! wǔshí kuài.
turns.text_en: This scarf is very pretty! Fifty yuan.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Try to negotiate a lower price.

options.text_zh: 晚上七点吧，我们六点半在电影院门口见？
options.text_pinyin: wǎnshang qī diǎn ba, wǒmen liù diǎn bàn zài diànyǐngyuàn ménkǒu jiàn?
options.text_en: Let's do 7 PM, meet at the cinema entrance at 6:30?
options.feedback: Off-topic — you were asked to try to negotiate a lower price, but this doesn't fit the conversation.

options.text_pinyin: tài guì le. sānshí kuài kěyǐ ma?
options.feedback: Classic haggling — state it's too expensive, then offer.

options.text_zh: 哇，成都看起来很漂亮！你去了哪些地方？
options.text_pinyin: wā, chéngdū kàn qǐlai hěn piàoliang! nǐ qù le nǎxiē dìfang?
options.text_en: Wow, Chengdu looks beautiful! What places did you visit?
options.feedback: Off-topic — you were asked to try to negotiate a lower price, but this doesn't fit the conversation.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_pinyin: sānshí tài shǎo le. sìshí kuài ba.
turns.text_en: Thirty is too little. How about forty?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Close the deal or counter again.

options.feedback: Nice counter. 行不行 is a natural way to propose.

options.text_zh: 我一直想去看大熊猫！火锅辣不辣？
options.text_pinyin: wǒ yīzhí xiǎng qù kàn dà xióngmāo! huǒguō là bú là?
options.text_en: I've always wanted to see the pandas! Was the hotpot spicy?
options.feedback: Off-topic — you were asked to close the deal or counter again, but this doesn't fit the conversation.

options.text_zh: 好啊，我很想去！你想看几点的？
options.text_pinyin: hǎo a, wǒ hěn xiǎng qù! nǐ xiǎng kàn jǐ diǎn de?
options.text_en: Sure, I'd love to go! What time do you want to see?
options.feedback: Off-topic — you were asked to close the deal or counter again, but this doesn't fit the conversation.

tree.cultural_note: Haggling is expected at Chinese street markets. Start at about 50-60% of the asking price. 太贵了 (tài guì le) is your best friend. Fixed-price shops don't haggle.
